title: Amortizing Maximum Inner Product Search with Learned Support Functions
gist: Apple proposes amortized MIPS: training neural networks (SupportNet and KeyNet) to directly predict maximum-inner-product-search solutions for queries from a known distribution, improving retrieval match rates per unit compute on the BEIR benchmark.

insights.claim: Maximum inner product search can be amortized by training neural networks to directly predict its solutions for queries drawn from a known distribution over a fixed key database, replacing repeated exact search.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Retrieval is a core cost center for RAG and vector-DB workloads; amortizing it cuts recurring inference compute where query distributions are stable.
insights.quote: a regression-based approach that trains neural networks to directly predict MIPS solutions, amortizing the cost of repeatedly solving MIPS for queries drawn from a known distribution over a fixed key database

insights.claim: The MIPS value function is the support function of the set of keys, a convex function whose gradient yields the optimal key, providing the theoretical basis for learning it.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Grounding retrieval in convex-analysis structure is what makes the learned approximation principled rather than heuristic.
insights.quote: the MIPS value function is the support function of the set of keys, a well-studied convex function whose gradient yields the optimal key

insights.claim: SupportNet is an input-convex neural network that regresses the support function and can act as a cluster router steering queries to relevant database partitions, while KeyNet directly regresses the optimal key and serves as a drop-in query replacement for off-the-shelf indexing pipelines.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: KeyNet's drop-in compatibility means gains can be captured without rearchitecting existing vector-search infrastructure.
insights.quote: KeyNet can be used as a drop-in replacement for the original query, fed directly to off-the-shelf indexing pipelines
insights.locator: SupportNet can serve as a cluster router

insights.claim: On the BEIR benchmark, learned SupportNets and KeyNets significantly improve IVF match rates for document embeddings when accounting for compute effort measured in FLOPs, number of probes, or wall-clock time.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Compute-normalized retrieval gains matter for search-at-scale economics; the code is open-sourced, easing adoption.
insights.quote: learned SupportNets and KeyNets significantly improve IVF match rates when accounting for compute effort, whether measured in FLOPs, number of probes, or wall-clock time
insights.locator: Our experiments on the BEIR benchmark
